name: Sik Do Rak
tagline: All-you-can-eat Korean BBQ open until 4am on Keeaumoku Street — the heart of Honolulu's late-night Koreatown scene.
mustOrder: AYCE galbi — ~$20/person for 90 minutes of unlimited meat
bulgogi and pork belly combo — the crowd favorites
banchan spread — replenished as fast as you can eat it
parking: Samsung Plaza lot on Keeaumoku St; street parking
talkStory: Keeaumoku Street is Honolulu's unofficial Koreatown — a strip of Korean BBQ joints, karaoke bars, and noodle shops that tourists almost never visit. Sik Do Rak is the anchor: all-you-can-eat Korean BBQ at about $20/person with a 90-minute time limit, open until 4am every night. The quality-to-price ratio is hard to beat, and the 4am closing time makes it the default destination for anyone who's hungry after the bars close. The banchan is solid, the ventilation could be better, and your clothes will smell like galbi for days. Worth it.
